frameworks.name: event segmentation theory
frameworks.one_line: sequences of event models gate switching of active representations during activity comprehension

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference over event types
frameworks.one_line: posterior over event schemas governs event-to-event transitions

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network dynamics
frameworks.one_line: RNN captures short-term within-event perceptual dynamics under each schema

frameworks.name: prediction-error gating
frameworks.one_line: alternative hypothesis that large prediction error triggers event boundaries

core_question: Does prediction uncertainty or prediction error better gate switching between event models during human activity comprehension?
key_assumptions: comprehension proceeds via sequences of discrete event models
event schemas can be learned from naturalistic activity
either uncertainty or error can serve as a gating signal for model updating
manipulations: gating signal variant: prediction uncertainty vs prediction error
training on naturalistic 18-hour activity corpus, tested on 3.5 hours held-out
key_findings: the uncertainty-gated variant best matched human segmentation
the uncertainty-gated variant formed event categories most like humans
interpretations: prediction uncertainty is a more plausible gating mechanism for event-model updating than prediction error
combining RNN dynamics with Bayesian schema inference scales event comprehension to naturalistic timescales
